safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live 2025-05-26 — the $0.95–$1.00 'Danger Zone' on the chart, labelled the strongest resistance + bearish liquidity + psychological level. This is a sell/short zone (take-profit-and-short), NOT a buy. Number is the low edge of that zone; full zone is $0.95–$1.00.

targets.basis: zero_live 2025-05-26 — $0.76 HTF demand/liquidity (0.79 fib); chart shows it was reached at ~$0.79.
zero_live chart — $0.55 'ideal zone to expect a bounce' (0.5 fib); deeper buy/cover zone shown on image but omitted from extracted text (estimated as a secondary cover level).

safest_plan.stop_basis: A daily close above the third drive / top of the Danger Zone (~$1.00) invalidates the short — chart notes the next target then becomes $1.20.

stance_by_source.summary: Treats $0.95–$1.00 as a take-profit-and-short zone, expecting a drop into the $0.76 demand area; graded a partial win that hit ~$0.79. Educational, not advice.

conflicts: The extracted text framed '0.95–1.00' as an entry, which reads like a buy; the chart makes clear it is the opposite — a short/take-profit zone ('TP IS A MUST, SHORT ALSO'). The image wins: this is a bearish setup, not a long entry.

The chart shows deeper buy zones the text left out — a $0.55 bounce zone and a $0.45–$0.47 'HTF demand / Fib extension' zone where Zero said to 'bet' if $0.55 fails. None were carried in the levels text.
plain_english: Only one source covers this — a Zero teaching chart from late May 2025, over a year old. Its message was that the $0.95–$1.00 area is a place to take profit and possibly bet on the price falling toward about $0.76, not a place to buy; that move already partly happened. Because it is a single, very old, educational call that has already played out, there is no fresh, agreed setup to act on. Stand aside and wait for a current read before doing anything.
